context_note: France vs Iraq - WC2026 Group D, Philadelphia. France are massive favorites (Elo FTR: FRA 81.5% / Draw 12.8% / IRQ 5.7%). Elo diff = 392.5 — LARGER than ARG-AUT (304.5), making Iraq an even more extreme underdog. RULE7 fires hard (IRQ 5.7% < 15%). RULE8 does NOT fire (draw 12.8% < 25%). GD1 DATA NOW CONFIRMED (see gd1_evidence). France scored all 3 goals after 66' vs Senegal; Iraq had only 1 SOT in 90' vs Norway. These are critical calibration inputs that revised MULTIPLE estimates from the preliminary pass.

elo_ratings.comparison: FRA-IRQ elo_diff (392.5) > ARG-AUT elo_diff (304.5) — Iraq is a bigger underdog than Austria was

gd1_evidence.fra_vs_sen_result: France 3-1 Senegal (all 3 France goals after 66th minute)

fra_first_half_only.note: BOMBSHELL: France were abysmal in 1H. Mbappe had only 14 touches. All 3 goals came 66-90+. Mbappe career WC: 12 2H goals vs only 1 1H goal.

gd1_evidence.irq_vs_nor_result: Iraq 1-4 Norway (Iraq scored in 39', then conceded late)

irq_vs_nor_stats.note: DEVASTATING: Iraq had only 1 SOT in 90 minutes total. ZERO shots after the 63rd minute. Iraq scored via counter (39') then defensive collapse: 3 goals conceded in 2H.

key_discipline_notes.irq_tahseen_yellow: Tahseen YC vs Norway (86') — one yellow, no ban
key_discipline_notes.zaid_ismael_risk: Racing Post tip: Ismael 3 YC in 7 caps, 10 bookings in 30 club appearances. Bet365 2/1 him to be carded.
key_discipline_notes.fra_tchouameni_out: Tchouameni did not train pre-match. Manu Kone starts instead.
key_discipline_notes.irq_ali_jasim: Neck knock, game-time decision, likely to start per RotoWire.

external_market_data.source_note: Betfair, bet365, CBS, Racing Post, FanDuel, Goal.com — all pre-match as of 2026-06-22
external_market_data.weather_risk: Philadelphia flood watch, 65% storm probability at kickoff (5pm ET). FIFA halts for lightning within 8 miles. Gates delayed. FanDuel explicitly passed on volume props. ALL COUNTING STATS (SOT, corners, fouls, cards) could be affected by stoppage/delay.

referee.name: Drew Fischer (Canada)
referee.assessment: Fischer is SIGNIFICANTLY above WC2026 tournament average. Higher card rate directly boosts Q3 probability (Iraq 1+ card in 2H).

rules_in_effect.RULE7: IRQ 5.7% win prob (< 15%) = EXTREME underdog. Stay near model for all IRQ-positive props. Do not allow crowd compression.
rules_in_effect.RULE15: Fires for any Iraq forward/player SOT question. No individual player prop in this batch.
rules_in_effect.RULE1_PENDING: Q6 France win: Smarkets not yet pulled (quota constraint). Market signals at 92-94% used instead. Preliminary 0.88.

ARG_AUT_LESSONS.france_1H_slow_start: CONFIRMED BY GD1: France 0 SOT in 1H vs Senegal. Directly lowers Q9 (France scores 1H) from 0.70 to 0.60.
ARG_AUT_LESSONS.iraq_sot_devastation: CONFIRMED BY GD1: Iraq 1 SOT in 90' vs Norway. Directly lowers Q5 (Iraq 2+ SOT in 2H) to 0.15 and Q8 (Iraq 4+ SOT) to 0.08.
ARG_AUT_LESSONS.blowout_vs_competitive: France vs Iraq expected to be a BLOWOUT (Elo diff 392.5 >> 2-goal expected margin). Apply blowout script: Iraq gives up = fewer cards, fewer Iraq SOT. BUT referee Fischer is card-happy which partially offsets.
ARG_AUT_LESSONS.corner_caution: Q1 is HALFTIME corners — at HT France will still be actively pressing. No chasing-team corner reversal applies at HT. HT corner is safe to push extreme.

Q1_iraq_more_ht_corners.question: At halftime, will Iraq have more corner kicks than France?
Q1_iraq_more_ht_corners.method: Poisson HT corners: lambda_FRA_HT=3.16, lambda_IRQ_HT=0.76. P(IRQ>FRA)=5.7%. RULE7 fires. AUT-JOR confirmed: extreme underdog gets fewer corners. France had 3 corners in 1H vs Senegal; Iraq only 3 total in 90' vs Spain.
Q1_iraq_more_ht_corners.gd1_update: GD1 confirms France win 3 HT corners vs Senegal. Iraq-Norway: Iraq corner count unclear but Iraq only created 10 shots — very few would generate corners vs deep-block. No reason to change.

alt_data.market_signal: Total corners over 7.5 at -315 (France dominant); 'each team 2+ corners' at -150
alt_data.note: France corners over 7.5 at -110 — market expects France to generate the bulk of corners

Q1_iraq_more_ht_corners.revision_note: Revised 0.08 → 0.07. GD1 France had 3 HT corners (vs 0 SOT) — France win corners even when poor. Iraq physically incapable of generating HT corners vs France.

Q2_france_more_fouls_than_iraq.question: Will France commit more fouls than Iraq?
Q2_france_more_fouls_than_iraq.gd1_update: CONFLICTING DATA: France 8 fouls vs Senegal 5 (France committed MORE). But Iraq committed 10-12 when defending deep (vs Norway 12, vs Spain 10). Pattern: Iraq as underdog defender fouls 10-12; France as attacking team 8-10. Iraq fouling more is slightly more likely.

alt_data.key_tension: France pressed and fouled more than Senegal (8-5). But Iraq's survival-mode defending generates 10-12 fouls. Which team hits 10+ vs the other's 8-10?

Q2_france_more_fouls_than_iraq.edge_direction: NOW: slight NO lean — Iraq fouls more when defending deep (10-12 vs France's likely 8-10)
Q2_france_more_fouls_than_iraq.revision_note: Revised 0.55 → 0.45. France pressed and fouled 8 times vs Senegal. But Iraq is in DEEPER defensive survival mode and committed 10-12 fouls in both GD1 and Spain friendly. On balance Iraq more likely to foul more.

Q3_iraq_1plus_card_2h.question: Will Iraq receive at least 1 card in the second half?
Q3_iraq_1plus_card_2h.gd1_update: STRONG UPWARD REVISION: (1) Tahseen YC in 86' vs Norway = Iraq DID get a second-half card in GD1. (2) Fischer averages 3.35-3.6 YC/game vs tournament average 2.17. (3) Ismael = 3 cards in 7 caps, Racing Post explicit tip. (4) Iraq tires in 2H and makes cynical fouls (Norway: 0 shots after 63', but still fouled and got carded in 86').

alt_data.irq_2h_card_vs_norway: Tahseen YC 86th minute — Iraq got a 2H card in GD1
alt_data.ismael_booking_risk: 3 cards in 7 caps; 10 bookings in 30 club apps; bet365 2/1 for a card
alt_data.iraq_fatigue_pattern: After 63' vs Norway: 0 shots, still fouling, carded in 86'

Q3_iraq_1plus_card_2h.edge_direction: YES lean: GD1 direct confirmation + Fischer above-average rate + Ismael risk
Q3_iraq_1plus_card_2h.revision_note: Revised 0.50 → 0.65. THREE independent signals all point YES: (1) Iraq literally got a 2H card in GD1. (2) Fischer is a stricter-than-average referee. (3) Ismael is extremely card-prone. This is now a confident lean.

Q4_both_score_and_3plus_goals.question: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
Q4_both_score_and_3plus_goals.method: Double Poisson P(both AND 3+) = 30.2% raw model. Revised down to 0.24 for extreme underdog.
Q4_both_score_and_3plus_goals.gd1_update: BTTS market at 36-42% (higher than our model). France failed to keep clean sheet in 6 straight. Iraq scored in GD1 (39' vs Norway) and vs Spain (28'). However BTTS No is the market favourite (-200). Iraq's 1 SOT in 90' vs Norway is concerning for their ability to score.

alt_data.france_clean_sheet_streak: No clean sheet in last 6 matches
alt_data.iraq_gd1_goal: Iraq scored in 39' vs Norway (1H counter/header)
alt_data.iraq_sot_context: Iraq had only 1 SOT in 90' vs Norway — scoring despite minimal SOT = their 1 chance went in (efficient)

Q4_both_score_and_3plus_goals.edge_direction: Slight upward revision — market BTTS 36-42% above our original 24%. But binding constraint = Iraq scores.
Q4_both_score_and_3plus_goals.revision_note: Revised 0.24 → 0.28. Market BTTS at 36-42% is above our model. France no clean sheet in 6. But BTTS No is still the market favourite. Middle ground: P(Iraq scores) ~32-35%, P(3+ total | Iraq scores) ~85%. ~0.28.

Q5_iraq_2plus_sot_2h.question: Will Iraq have 2 or more shots on target in the second half?
Q5_iraq_2plus_sot_2h.method: lambda_IRQ_SOT_2H (x3.3)=0.990, P(>=2)=26.1%. RULE7 pushed down to 0.22.
Q5_iraq_2plus_sot_2h.gd1_update: DEVASTATING DOWNWARD REVISION: Iraq had only 1 SOT in ALL 90 MINUTES vs Norway. Zero shots after the 63rd minute. Norway are weaker than France defensively (Upamecano/Saliba vs Norway's CBs). Getting 2+ SOT specifically in just the 2H when they couldn't get 2 in 90' total vs a weaker team = extremely unlikely.

alt_data.note: Spain data was with a weakened squad. Norway data is most relevant (competitive, current team).

Q5_iraq_2plus_sot_2h.edge_direction: STRONG NO: GD1 shows Iraq barely shots on target at all
Q5_iraq_2plus_sot_2h.revision_note: Revised 0.22 → 0.15. Iraq 1 SOT in 90' vs Norway is the decisive data point. France are BETTER defensively than Norway. Getting 2 SOT in just the 2H when they got 1 total vs a weaker team is a very low probability event. RULE7 + GD1 combined.

Q6_france_win.question: Will France win the match?
Q6_france_win.method: Elo model 81.5%. RULE1: avg(crowd, Smarkets) pending.
Q6_france_win.gd1_update: MAJOR UPWARD REVISION: Multiple sharp signals converge above our model. France won 3-1 vs Senegal (form confirmation). Iraq lost 1-4 to Norway (confirmation of fragility). Market moved significantly above our model.

alt_data.france_gd1: Won 3-1, strong performance despite slow 1H start
alt_data.iraq_gd1: Lost 1-4, only 1 SOT in 90 minutes — complete defensive collapse
alt_data.head_to_head: FIRST EVER meeting between France and Iraq

Q6_france_win.edge_direction: YES: Market 90-93% >> our model 81.5%. GD1 confirms gap. Submit high.
Q6_france_win.revision_note: Revised 0.81 → 0.88. Opta at 88.5%, sharp markets at 92-94%, Iraq 1-4 collapse in GD1. Our Elo model is conservative and doesn't capture form/lineup dynamics. 0.88 is the convergence point between our model (81.5%) and sharp market signals (90-93%).

Q7_iraq_scores_2h.question: Will Iraq score in the second half?
Q7_iraq_scores_2h.gd1_update: SIGNIFICANT DOWNWARD REVISION: Iraq's goal vs Norway came in 39' (1H). Iraq's goal vs Spain came in 28' (1H). Iraq's pattern is FIRST HALF goals only — counter/set-piece early when fresh. After 63' vs Norway: zero shots. Iraq's 2H attacking output collapses completely under sustained pressure.

alt_data.late_consolation_precedent: Senegal scored 90+5' vs France (garbage time) when France were at 2-1 up. This template applies here.
alt_data.btts_market: Iraq total scoring probability ~35-40% (via BTTS market)

Q7_iraq_scores_2h.edge_direction: Strong NO lean: pattern is all Iraq goals come in 1H. 2H goals extremely rare vs top opponents.
Q7_iraq_scores_2h.revision_note: Revised 0.26 → 0.18. Both Iraq goals in this tournament (vs Norway) and recent (vs Spain) came in the first half. Iraq has zero second-half attacking output under sustained pressure. ~35-40% total Iraq scoring probability × ~25% 2H share ≈ 0.09-0.10 base, plus ~8% late consolation (garbage time) = ~0.17-0.18.

Q8_iraq_4plus_sot.question: Will Iraq have 4 or more shots on target?
Q8_iraq_4plus_sot.gd1_update: CRITICAL DOWNWARD REVISION: Iraq had 1 SOT in 90 minutes vs Norway. 4+ SOT would be 4× their GD1 output against a better defensive team. This is essentially off the table barring a chaos scenario.

alt_data.france_def_better_than_norway: Upamecano + Saliba > Norway's CB pairing. France also had 8 SOT vs Senegal — disciplined defensive unit.
alt_data.goalkeeper_projection: FanDuel projects France keeper Maignan for 5 saves — implying 5 Iraq shots in total, at maybe 30-40% on target rate = ~1-2 SOT total

Q8_iraq_4plus_sot.edge_direction: EXTREME NO: Iraq 1 SOT in 90' vs weaker opponent. This is our single strongest edge of the batch.
Q8_iraq_4plus_sot.revision_note: Revised 0.14 → 0.08. Iraq had 1 SOT in 90' vs Norway (weaker than France). 4+ SOT vs France would require them to 4× their recent best. This is essentially impossible short of a bizarre game-state. This is the HIGHEST CONFIDENCE position in the batch — push hard NO.

Q9_france_scores_1h.question: Will France score in the first half?
Q9_france_scores_1h.method: lambda_FRA_1H=1.016, P(scores)=63.8%. Revised UP to 0.70 based on pre-tournament form.
Q9_france_scores_1h.gd1_update: DOWNWARD REVISION: France had 1 total shot, 0 on target, 0.02 xG in 1H vs Senegal. All 3 goals came after 66'. Deschamps' system is historically slow-starting. Mbappe has only 1 career WC goal before HT vs 12 after. Pre-tournament form (scored in 1H of last 3 wins) less relevant than actual GD1 data.

alt_data.tension: France slow starters (0.02 xG in 1H vs Senegal) vs Iraq far weaker than Senegal defensively

Q9_france_scores_1h.edge_direction: GD1 data pulls strongly toward NO (0 SOT in 1H vs Senegal is alarming). Iraq weakness partially offsets.
Q9_france_scores_1h.revision_note: Revised 0.70 → 0.60. France's GD1 1H was nearly goalless despite eventually winning 3-1. This is a repeatable pattern for Deschamps' France (methodical build, second-half surge). But Iraq are much weaker than Senegal defensively, and Norway scored in 29' vs Iraq. Best estimate: 60% that France score in 1H. Submit 0.60 not 0.70.

Q1.notes: STRONG NO. Model 5.7% + RULE7 + France had 3 HT corners vs Senegal + Iraq only 3 total corners vs Spain. Iraq cannot win HT corner count vs France.

Q2.notes: Near coin-flip revised slightly toward NO. Iraq fouls 10-12 when defending deep (Norway, Spain). France fouls ~8-10 as pressing attacker. Iraq may foul more.

Q3.notes: Revised UP from 0.50. Fischer 3.35 YC/game (above WC avg 2.17) + Ismael card-prone + Iraq LITERALLY got a 2H card (86') in GD1 vs Norway. Three independent YES signals.

Q4.tier: TIER 3 — slight YES lean vs model
Q4.notes: Revised from 0.24 to 0.28. BTTS market 36-42%. France no clean sheet in 6. But BTTS No is still the market favourite. Iraq scored once in GD1 despite only 1 SOT.

Q5.notes: STRONG NO. Iraq had 1 total SOT in 90' vs Norway. Zero shots after 63'. France are better defensively. Getting 2+ SOT specifically in 2H is essentially impossible based on GD1 evidence.

Q6.notes: Revised UP from 0.81. Market 92-94%, Opta 88.5%. France GD1 win confirmed, Iraq GD1 collapse confirmed. 0.88 is midpoint between our model (81.5%) and sharp markets (90-93%).

Q7.notes: Revised DOWN from 0.26. Both Iraq goals in tournament play came in 1H (39' vs Norway, 28' vs Spain). Iraq's 2H output: 0 shots after 63'. Rare late consolation scenario is the main residual probability.

Q8.notes: EXTREME NO. HIGHEST CONFIDENCE estimate in this batch. Iraq 1 SOT in 90' vs Norway. 4× that vs a BETTER defensive team = essentially impossible. RULE7 + devastating GD1 evidence.

Q9.notes: Revised DOWN from 0.70. France had 0 SOT in 1H vs Senegal; all 3 goals after 66'. Mbappe 1 career WC 1H goal vs 12 2H goals. Iraq weak defense partially offsets but GD1 pattern is strong.

edge_ranking.2_Q1_iraq_ht_corners: 0.07 — extreme NO, France press dominates corners from minute 1
edge_ranking.3_Q5_iraq_2plus_sot_2h: 0.15 — strong NO, Iraq literally had 0 shots after 63' in GD1
edge_ranking.4_Q3_iraq_1plus_card_2h: 0.65 — YES lean, Fischer + Ismael + GD1 2H card confirmed
edge_ranking.5_Q6_france_win: 0.88 — YES, strong market consensus above our model
edge_ranking.6_Q7_iraq_scores_2h: 0.18 — NO, all Iraq goals are 1H in tournament play
edge_ranking.7_Q9_france_scores_1h: 0.60 — moderate YES, GD1 slow start partially offset by Iraq weakness

edge_ranking.9_Q2_france_more_fouls: 0.45 — near coin-flip, Iraq fouls more when defending deep

recovery_context.recovery_plan: Push hard on TIER 1 questions (Q8, Q1, Q5, Q6) where confidence is highest. Avoid ARG-AUT mistake of applying wrong match script (blowout vs competitive). FRA-IRQ expected to be a blowout (Elo 392.5 gap) — apply BLOWOUT script: Iraq gives up, fewer Iraq SOT, corners go to France, Iraq tired = cards late.

arg_aut_lessons_applied.Q1: HT corner question — blowout script: France dominates corners from first whistle
arg_aut_lessons_applied.Q5_Q8: Iraq SOT devastatingly low in GD1 (1 in 90') — confirms BLOWOUT script: underdog gives up
arg_aut_lessons_applied.Q3: Cards: unlike ARG-AUT where cards came from AUT fighting hard (competitive), Iraq collapses in 2H but still fouls late = Fischer ensures at least 1 2H YC
arg_aut_lessons_applied.Q9: France 1H slow start — same Deschamps pattern as ARG-AUT (ARG scored but second half was when they added). France may replicate the 0-0 HT from GD1.
